_backend_doc: ue_physics = ArduPilot runs --model JSON and gets physics from UE's UVTOLPhysicsComponent using the mass/inertia/aero values below. Use this for simulating real vehicles with measured data. ap_native = ArduPilot's built-in QuadPlane model; ignores mechanical/electrical fields.

_doc.mass_kg: Total vehicle mass in kg — with full batteries, no payload. Used for F=ma and hover thrust calibration.
_doc.inertia_roll_kg_m2: Moment of inertia around the roll axis (X, wing-to-wing), in kg·m². Larger = slower roll response. Estimated from motor arm length and wing mass distribution.
_doc.inertia_pitch_kg_m2: Moment of inertia around the pitch axis (Y, nose-to-tail), in kg·m². Larger = slower pitch response.
_doc.inertia_yaw_kg_m2: Moment of inertia around the yaw axis (Z, vertical), in kg·m². Usually the largest of the three for a VTOL with wide wingspan.
_doc.arm_length_m: Distance from the vehicle centre to each lift motor, in metres. For a quad: half the motor-to-motor diagonal. Affects roll/pitch torque authority: bigger arm = more torque per unit thrust difference.
_doc.wing_area_m2: Total wing reference area in m². Used in all aerodynamic force calculations: L = 0.5 * rho * V^2 * S * CL.
_doc.aspect_ratio: Wing aspect ratio: span^2 / wing_area. Higher AR = more efficient wing (less induced drag). Typical values: 5–10 for UAVs.
_doc.oswald_e: Oswald efficiency factor (0–1). Accounts for non-ideal lift distribution across the span. 0.8 is typical for a clean UAV wing.
_doc.mac_m: Mean Aerodynamic Chord in metres — effective average chord length used in pitch moment calculations. For a tapered wing: MAC = (2/3) * root_chord * (1 + taper + taper^2) / (1 + taper).
_doc.cl0: Lift coefficient at zero angle of attack. Positive for cambered airfoils (produces lift even at 0° AoA). Symmetric airfoils have CL0 = 0.
_doc.cl_alpha: Lift curve slope in 1/radian (dCL/dAlpha). Finite-wing value ≈ 2*pi / (1 + 2/AR). Typical range: 4–6 /rad for UAV wings.
_doc.cd0: Zero-lift (parasite) drag coefficient. Includes fuselage, landing gear, motor nacelles. Typical small UAV: 0.02–0.05.
_doc.cy_beta: Side-force derivative dCY/dBeta in 1/radian. Negative for a stable aircraft (sideslip creates restoring side force). Typical: -0.2 to -0.5.
_doc.cm_aileron: Roll moment coefficient per radian of aileron deflection (dCl/dDelta_a). Positive = right aileron down → right roll. Typical: 0.1–0.4.
_doc.cm_elevator: Pitch moment coefficient per radian of elevator deflection (dCm/dDelta_e). Negative = trailing-edge-down elevator → nose-up moment. Typical: -0.5 to -1.5.
_doc.cm_rudder: Yaw moment coefficient per radian of rudder deflection (dCn/dDelta_r). Positive = trailing-edge-right rudder → nose-right yaw. Typical: 0.05–0.2.
_doc.cm_alpha: Static pitch stiffness dCm/dAlpha in 1/radian. MUST be negative (statically stable) — without it the autopilot pitch loop diverges (BUGFIXES 2026-06-10 PM2). ≈ -cl_alpha × static margin; -0.7 with cl_alpha 4.5 ≈ 15% margin. Replace with wind-tunnel/AVL data when available.
_doc.cm_q: Pitch rate damping dCm/dq̂ per radian, q̂ = q·MAC/(2V). Negative. Typical: -8 to -25.
_doc.cl_p: Roll rate damping dCl/dp̂ per radian, p̂ = p·b/(2V). Negative. Typical: -0.4 to -0.6.
_doc.cn_r: Yaw rate damping dCn/dr̂ per radian, r̂ = r·b/(2V). Negative. Typical: -0.1 to -0.2.
_doc.cn_beta: Weathervane stability dCn/dBeta in 1/radian. Positive = nose turns into the relative wind. Typical: 0.05–0.15.
_doc.stall_aoa_deg: Angle of attack at which the wing stalls (loses lift rapidly), in degrees. Above this, CL is clamped to prevent runaway. Typical: 10–16° for UAV airfoils.
_doc.min_airspeed_ms: Airspeed below which all aerodynamic forces are suppressed (m/s). During VTOL hover the airspeed is near zero and aero forces would be noise — suppress them until the vehicle is flying fast enough for aero to matter.
_doc.max_control_deflection_deg: Maximum control surface deflection angle in degrees (applies to aileron, elevator, rudder). PWM 1000/2000 maps to ±this angle.
_doc.gravity_ms2: Gravitational acceleration in m/s². 9.81 at sea level. Rarely needs changing.
_doc.air_density: Air density in kg/m³. 1.225 at sea level, 15°C. Reduce for high-altitude operation (e.g. 1.112 at 1000 m, 1.007 at 2000 m).
